CR-101.reason: Access credentials rotation with potential deployment pipeline impact

CR-102.reason: Adding an index is a standard database optimization that improves query performance with minimal risk of breaking existing functionality.

CR-103.reason: Granting write access to production database to interns poses severe security and data integrity risks.

CR-104.reason: Minor patch version update with no breaking changes or new dependencies

CR-105.reason: Disabling audit logging compromises security monitoring and compliance requirements for an extended period.

CR-106.reason: Adds resilience but could introduce latency spikes and complexity in error handling if not properly implemented.

CR-107.reason: Self-approval of code changes bypasses review process and poses security and quality control risks.

CR-108.reason: Simple timeout adjustment that only increases wait time, no functional changes or potential for breaking existing behavior.
